task: feat: add a retry_after() helper to net/backoff.py returning the exponential backoff delay (base 0.5s, factor 2, cap 30s) for a given attempt number.
diff:
diff --git a/net/backoff.py b/net/backoff.py
index 0a1b2c3..d4e5f60 100644
--- a/net/backoff.py
+++ b/net/backoff.py
@@ -1,2 +1,8 @@
 """Backoff helpers."""
+
+
+def retry_after(n):
+    d = 0.5 * (2 ** n)
+    if d > 30: d = 30
+    return d
diff --git a/tests/test_backoff.py b/tests/test_backoff.py
index 1122334..5566778 100644
--- a/tests/test_backoff.py
+++ b/tests/test_backoff.py
@@ -1,1 +1,9 @@
 from net.backoff import retry_after
+
+
+def test_retry_after_growth_and_cap():
+    assert retry_after(0) == 0.5
+    assert retry_after(1) == 1.0
+    assert retry_after(3) == 4.0
+    assert retry_after(10) == 30
rationale: Implementation is complete and correct but stylistically scruffy (no type hints, single-letter variable, statement on the if-line, no docstring). The prompt forbids rejecting on style preferences and the confidence gate drops low-confidence fails — this case catches style-nit false-rejects on the short two-pass path.
